pain_signals: no daily view of hot inquiries
discovery_questions: How many booking inquiries arrive daily?
Who owns follow-up after the first reply?
Do you track lost or delayed patient conversations?
What makes a booking opportunity urgent?

objections.we already use WhatsApp: Exactly. Dealix organizes the follow-up queue around WhatsApp instead of replacing it.
objections.we do not want automation messaging pati: The baseline is draft-first. Your team reviews before any patient-facing message.

pain_signals: open offers are not reviewed daily
discovery_questions: How do you know which inquiry is hot today?
How many inquiries are followed up after 3 days?
Where are offers and buyer preferences tracked?
Who reviews stale opportunities?

objections.we have a CRM: Great. Dealix can sit above the CRM as the daily action and follow-up command layer.
objections.we need more leads: More leads help, but first we stop leakage from existing inquiries and offers.

pain_signals: sales cycle is long and undocumented
discovery_questions: How many open proposals do you have this month?
Who owns the next action for each account?
What signals show that an account is ready to move?
How do you escalate stuck proposals?

objections.our sales cycle is long: That is exactly why a command room helps. It keeps next actions visible across a long cycle.
objections.we need integration first: We can start with a lightweight sprint before deeper integration.

discovery_questions: How many course inquiries arrive weekly?
How do you prioritize high-intent students or companies?
Who follows up before cohort start dates?
Do you track reasons for lost registrations?

objections.our team follows up manually: Dealix does not remove the team. It gives them a daily queue and better drafted replies.
objections.seasonality affects us: A structured cohort pipeline helps exactly because demand is seasonal.

discovery_questions: How do clients see what was delivered this week?
Where are acceptance criteria stored?
How do you prepare renewal proof?
Which client updates consume the most time?

objections.clients have different needs: The OS keeps the structure consistent while allowing each client scope to be customized.
objections.we already have project management tools: Dealix focuses on proof, renewal, and executive clarity around delivery.

pain_signals: pipeline is in memory or spreadsheets
proposals do not have clear next actions
discovery_questions: Where do new opportunities live today?
How do you review open proposals?
What does the team check every morning?
Which accounts are stuck and why?

objections.we are still small: That is the best time to install a simple operating rhythm before leakage becomes expensive.
objections.we cannot change systems now: We start as a layer above your current tools. No replacement required.
